summary: v2.6c QR Verifikasi BASTK + POST /api/orders/{id}/convert + _odoo_sync_order stub — Full end-to-end testing PASSED. Backend: 13/13 new pytest in test_orders_v26c_convert.py + full regression 106/106 across all backend tests (previous 93 + 13 new). Frontend: /?bastk=TRIP-POD-DEMO renders QR Verifikasi panel correctly — QR SVG with center logo (AAL flag, level=H error correction) encodes ${origin}/?track=TRIP-POD-DEMO, caption 'SCAN UNTUK LACAK' present, all 5 metadata rows populated (No. BASTK, Trip ID, No. Polisi, Tanggal Cetak, Verifikasi URL). BASTK header doc-no format matches /^BASTK\/\d{6}\/[A-Z0-9]{6}$/ (actual: BASTK/202606/ODDEMO derived from 'TRIPPODDEMO'.slice(-6)). PDF download (btn-download-bastk) completes successfully with QR panel included, zero console errors. Odoo sync verified: backend log shows '[odoo:sync_order:skip] order_id=... (env not configured)' after each convert call, no exceptions raised. Version bumped: GET /api/ returns v='2.6c'.

issues: QR panel renders cleanly with Navy + Gold theme — QR SVG with AAL flag center logo, caption 'SCAN UNTUK LACAK' bold gold, 5 metadata rows aligned with monospace values, soft cream note below explaining digital verification. No contrast or alignment issues.

action_items: (NIT) Review-request spec says BASTK No tail is '6 hex' but actual code (BASTKPage.jsx:204) uses /[^A-Z0-9]/gi regex producing 6 alphanumeric (not strictly hex). Example output 'BASTK/202606/ODDEMO' contains non-hex letters (O, D). Either tighten regex to hex-only or update spec wording. Non-blocking — visually correct and consistent.
critical_code_review_comments: OrderConvertBody model accepts all optional payload (trip_id, driver_id, uj, t1, t2, t3, bonus_daily, bonus_kerajinan) with sensible defaults. Idempotency via order.trip_id check at server.py:687-697 — clean.

Trip pre-fill from order: route='asal_kota - tujuan_kota', nopol fallback 'TBD-<last4>', customer_data prefers tujuan_alamat/delivery_pic. source_order_id backlink added. All schema-additive — no breaking changes to existing trips.

_odoo_sync_order is fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task — won't block /convert response. Skips cleanly when OdooClient.enabled=False (env empty). Never raises (try/except wraps entire body). Verified in backend.err.log: '[odoo:sync_order:skip] order_id=... (env not configured)'.
409 collision check at server.py:702-704 prevents trip_id clobbering when admin passes a trip_id already used elsewhere.
QR generation uses qrcode.react@4.2.0 with level='H' (30% error correction) + 28x28 SVG logo embedded via excavate=true — keeps QR scannable even with logo overlay. Production-grade choice.

Frontend BASTK No derivation client-side from trip_id slice — deterministic + stable across reloads (no DB round-trip). Good for offline-friendly UX. However: tail uses alphanumeric not hex (minor spec mismatch noted in action_items).
Track URL uses window.location.origin so it auto-adapts to deployment domain. Clean.
MongoDB _id stripped from all responses via trip_doc_to_public(). No serialization leaks.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_orders_v26c_convert.py (NEW — 13 tests: convert happy path x5, errors x2, odoo sync stub x2, version, regression x3)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on QR Verifikasi panel + PDF download + zero console errors

seed_data_creation: Created ~10 TEST_-prefixed orders + TEST_TRIP_* + TEST_TRIP_COLLIDE_* + TRIP-ORD-* trips during convert/idempotent testing. Clean via: db.orders.deleteMany({customer_nama: /^TEST_/}); db.trips.deleteMany({trip_id: /^(TEST_|TRIP-ORD-)/}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.6c is STABLE. Adds: (1) QR Verifikasi panel inside BASTK print area (qrcode.react@4.2.0, level=H, center logo) encoding /?track=<trip_id>; (2) POST /api/orders/{id}/convert idempotent endpoint creating trip + setting order.status=DISPATCHED; (3) _odoo_sync_order best-effort XML-RPC sync after convert (env-gated, no-op when ODOO_URL empty). Backend version='2.6c'. BASTK No format: BASTK/YYYYMM/<6 alphanumeric uppercase derived from trip_id.replace(/[^A-Z0-9]/gi).slice(-6)> — note spec says 'hex' but actual is alphanumeric. /?bastk=TRIP-POD-DEMO is the demo route. 106/106 backend tests pass. No regressions from v2.6b.
rca of the issue: No issues found in v2.6c. Single nit: spec wording 'BASTK/YYYYMM/<6 hex>' is slightly imprecise — actual implementation produces 6 alphanumeric uppercase (matches /^[A-Z0-9]{6}$/ not /^[A-F0-9]{6}$/). For demo trip 'TRIP-POD-DEMO' tail = 'ODDEMO'. Code at BASTKPage.jsx:204 is correct and deterministic; spec docs could be tightened.
